name: Workbook visual design system
intent: Codify color coordination, visual hierarchy, and artistic workbook choices so generated spreadsheets do not reinvent presentation rules.

principles.function_first: Never change formulas, row contracts, or source data behavior during a style pass.
principles.executive_scan_first: Executive tabs must support fast visual scanning before detailed editing.
principles.semantic_color_only: Color must communicate state, role, or hierarchy. Do not decorate randomly.
principles.calm_by_default: Use slate, charcoal, muted blue-gray, and soft fills as the normal presentation layer.
principles.red_is_expensive: Use red only for blockers, duplicate review, failed checks, or stop-ship items.
principles.amber_means_review: Use amber for review-needed states, not for routine rows.
principles.green_means_valid_or_source: Use green for confirmed/resolved rows or trusted source-entry surfaces.
principles.gray_means_quiet_or_evidence: Use gray for snapshots, archives, deprecated sheets, and quiet reference material.

inventory_visual_field.placement: Immediately after Total Qty in executive inventory rollup tables.
inventory_visual_field.implementation: In-cell REPT progression bar based on row quantity scaled against visible rollup max quantity.

anti_patterns: Bright traffic-light colors on routine rows
Separate charts replacing embedded executive visual fields
Workbook-visible claims that an artifact is Web Excel-safe
URL-encoded text such as %20 in workbook titles or visible cells
Sheet protection before the functional view is proven
Overwide dashboards where key fields are off-screen on first open
Hidden helper mechanics with no README or repo notes
acceptance_checks: Executive tab opens on a useful first-screen view
Required visual fields are visible without searching
The title is short and client-safe
The workbook contains no generated compatibility bragging text
Formula count and formula text are unchanged during style-only passes
Tab colors communicate each sheet's role
The artifact passes the operator's Excel for Web review before it is called successful
